observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to require the: (a) Property-in-Charge to: (i) collaborate with all heads of local offices for the reorganization documentation of PPEs with the end view of completing the physical count of PPEs; and (ii) cause the registration with the LTO of all serviceable vehicles of the Municipality; (b) Inventory Committee to prioritize the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as per COA Circular No. 2020-006 to address the unreconciled reciprocal records of the Property and Accounting Units; (c) Disposal Committee to facilitate the identification and disposition of unserviceable properties in conformance with the prescribed procedures; and (d) Municipal Accountant and Property-in-Charge to: (i) continue completing and updating PPELCs and property cards, respectively; and (ii) regularly reconcile their PPE records.
observations.managementAction: Requesting for documentation The LGU are still trying to locate old files of PPE reports and schedules. Requesting for additional personnel Creation and funding of additional personnel to handle the updating of PPELCs and PCs. To establish a Disposal Committee to handle the request for the disposal of unserviceable PPE items. Registration of serviceable vehicles were done.
observations.reason: The LGU has yet to start planning for one-time cleansing of PPE account due to lack of personnel. The LGU had yet to finalize the disposal documents process for the unserviceable properties. Lack of manpower and funding for completion and updating of PPELCs and property cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to require the: (a) Municipal Accountant to: (i) coordinate with the depository bank to recapture the necessary information to support the adjustments for the unresolved items in the BRS; and (ii) revert to Cash-in-Bank account all stale checks which are no longer valid with reciprocal recognition of payable accounts through the following adjusting entry: Cash in Bank – Current Account ₱ 390,816.94; Accounts Payable ₱ 390,816.94; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to retrieve the records/history related to the dormant Trust Fund accounts to determine if any amount will be returned to the source agencies, otherwise cause the closure of the said inactive accounts or transfer its balances to an active General Fund account.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will send formal request and coordinate with LBP. The MTO effected the necessary adjustments. The MTO to request for SB Resolution to close the dormant Trust Fund accounts. The records of the dormant Trust Fund accounts were not yet finalized.
observations.reason: Formal request and copies of credit memos were not yet formally communicated with the depository. Results of review of the records of the dormant Trust Fund accounts were not yet finalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the: (a) accountable officers to immediately settle all their cash advances which are due for liquidation; (b) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries of accountable officers who have long outstanding balances; (c) Municipal Accountant to: (i) consistently issue demand letters to those who have not liquidated their cash advances on time; and (ii) initiate the request for write-off of unliquidated cash advances that are eligible for write off subject to COA rules and regulations; and (d) Management to take legal steps in the establishment of the official status of the missing AOs and make appropriate actions moving forward.
observations.managementAction: LCE instructed accountable officers to settle accountable officers immediate all their cash advances which are already due for liquidation; Majority of the accountable officers are no longer connected with the LGU; Accountable officers with a significant outstanding cash advance executed a notarized authority to deduct from his salary from 2025 to 2028. Municipal Accountant strictly enforced the rules in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances for the current year and preceding years. The Municipal Accountant will prepare and issue demand letters to erring employees; LGU will review the status of unliquidated cash advances to assess its eligibility to be included in the request for write-off. Legal Officer to initiate legal procedures to establish the official status of the missing AOs and to make appropriate actions moving forward.
observations.reason: Majority of accountable officers are no longer with the LGU (deceased, separated, unknown whereabouts). Demand letters had yet to be sent. Request for write-off of dormant cash advances was not yet prepared and submitted. Management deferred legal actions due to humanitarian reasons in some cases.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to direct the MSWMB to: (a) strengthen the role of the Barangays in solid waste management by strictly enforcing the composting operation in the MRFs and ensure that they are fully operational and functional to lessen the tipping fees paid by the Municipal government; (b) provide additional funds and personnel to operationalize the Central MRF and to help improve the capacity of the MENR Office to fulfill its mandate; and (c) cause the revision of the LGU’s 10-Year SWMP to include PPAs that are viable, realistic and suitable to the present condition and capability of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Request Not Implemented - additional funding for the next two years. Creation of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office and plantilla items was not implemented due to limitation on budget availability. The LGU is still in planning stages for formulation and implementation of the revised 10-year SWMP. Implementation of MRF improvements and central MRF operationalization await additional funding and creation of plantilla items.
observations.reason: Difficulty in formulation and implementation of planned activities due to limitations of budget in each barangay and lack of budgetary availability for creation of plantilla items and central MRF operationalization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: (a) provide the BPLO with the revenue performance targets set by the BLGF; (b) adopt and incorporate the said business tax targets in the LGU’s annual budget; and (c) collaborate with the BPLO in the planning and execution of intensive collection strategies geared to further increase its business tax revenues.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury exerted efforts ensuring that set target will be met as at year-end. The LGU will incorporate the set target in the budget; however, incorporation into the budget was not yet completed. Collaboration with BPLO ongoing; required to keep collaboration with revenue generating offices and TWG for strategic planning and execution to intensify collection. Additional manpower and ICT support requested but not yet funded.
observations.reason: Lack of funds and manpower delayed hiring of additional personnel and ICT upgrades; incorporation of business tax targets into the budget not yet effected; coordination and full implementation of intensified collection strategy still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to: (a) continue its commitment of adhering with the BIR regulations on the withholding and remittance of taxes in favor of the government; and (b) ensure its continuous efforts to remit the prior years’ outstanding amounts due to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer shall continue its commitment to uphold BIR regulations by promptly remitting all the taxes due and owing to the government. Assign or hire personnel who will focus on tracing and reconciliation of prior years’ outstanding balances (not yet implemented).
observations.reason: Prior years’ outstanding balances remain to be traced and reconciled; personnel to trace and reconcile not yet assigned/hired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Human Resource Officer in collaboration with the Municipal Accountant to: (a) continue the reconciliation of employee records with the GSIS EBCS; and (b) facilitate the coordination of former LGU employees and the GSIS in the settlement of the prior years’ unpaid contributions and loan amortizations.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled balance for the CYs 2022 to 2024. The LGU will send written communication to the last known address of other employees formerly separated from the agency to facilitate settlement of unpaid contributions and loans (not yet implemented).
observations.reason: Some former employees could not be located; communications to them for settlement of unpaid contributions and loan amortizations have not been completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant to: (a) remain compliant with the provisions of R.A. No. 9679 on the deduction and remittance of HDMF contributions and loan payments; and (b) cause the tracing and identification of the employees from whom such deductions were made to settle the unremitted HDMF prior years’ balances.
observations.managementAction: Strict compliance with HDMF regulations. Remit all deductions promptly. While waiting for additional manpower, an employee will be temporarily designated to handle the reconciliation of unsettled HDMF balances of prior years; reconciliation has yet to be completed.
observations.reason: Lack of funds to hire additional manpower caused delay in completing reconciliation of prior years’ unremitted HDMF balances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to require
the (a) Property-in-Charge to (i) collaborate with all heads of local offices
for the documentation of PPEs with the end view of completing the
physical count of PPEs; and (ii) facilitate the registration with the LTO of
all serviceable vehicles of the Municipality; (b) Inventory Committee to
prioritize the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances as per COA
Circular No. 2020-006 to address the unreconciled reciprocal records of
the Property and Accounting Units; (c) Disposal Committee to facilitate
the identification and disposition of unserviceable properties in
conformance with the prescribed procedures; and (d) Municipal
Accountant and Property-in-Charge to (i) complete and update PPELCs
and property cards, respectively; and (ii) regularly reconcile their PPE
records.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to (i) coordinate with the depository bank to obtain the
necessary information to support the adjustments for the unresolved
Credit Memos in the BRS; and (ii) revert to Cash-in-Bank account the
stale check with reciprocal recognition of payable account through the
following adjusting entry:

Cash in Bank – Current Account ₱ 16,268
Accounts Payable ₱ 16,268
To record the cancellation of stale check

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the Municipal
Accountant: (a) promptly recognize and record all leave benefits payable,
including those for employees who have separated from government
service in accordance with the provisions outlined in DBM Budget Circular
No. 2013-01, COA Circular Nos. 2024-009 and 2012-001, and P.D. No. 1445;
(b) work closely with the HRMO and the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure
that updated leave cards for all active employees are reconciled and adequate
appropriations for leave credits are allocated; (c) ensure that the disbursement
vouchers are supported with complete documentation in compliance with
Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14,
2012. Moreover, we recommend that Management comply with the
transitional provisions of the Circular and adopt a plan to gradually recognize
leave benefits payable to ensure the completeness and accuracy of financial
reports.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the (a) Municipal
Accountant to (i) effect the necessary adjustments to fully record the
RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax
Due (CLTTD) at the beginning of the year; and (ii) make the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the identified misstatements between the RPT
Receivable and Deferred Income accounts; and (b) Municipal Treasurer
(i) ensure the timely preparation and updating of the CLTTD and provide
the same to the Accounting Office; (ii) implement assertive collection
strategies and remedial measures to improve the Municipality’s tax
collection performance; and (iii) spearhead the collection activities
involving the barangay officials in the tax campaign in order to reach out
to a larger area.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the (a) accountable
officers to settle immediately all their cash advances which are already
due for liquidation; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) strictly enforce the
rules in the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances cited in
COA Circular No. 97-002; (ii) issue demand letters to those who have not
liquidated their cash advances; and (iii) submit a request for write-off of
dormant cash advances to the COA; and (c) Legal Officer to initiate legal
procedures to establish the official status of the missing AOs and to make
appropriate actions moving forward.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer
in collaboration with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to
(a) ensure that the 20% DF is properly and optimally utilized; and (b)
program development projects catering to the socio-economic and
environmental needs of the Municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MDRRMO to
continue the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF through consistent
assessment and implementation of PPAs that enables the Municipality to
sustain its resilience in responding and mitigating disasters and
calamities.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to (a) direct the BPLO and MTO
to closely coordinate and work collaboratively to ensure the continuous
achievement and surpassing of revenue targets; and (b) provide
additional resources to the BPLO to further strengthen and sustain the
execution of effective policies towards the attainment of regulatory
revenue targets.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the (a) Human
Resource Management Officer to prepare and maintain personnel files,
particularly contract of services with clearly defined duties and
responsibilities of hired Job Order Personnel to avoid confusion and
duplication of duties; and (b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the
supporting documents were attached to the disbursement vouchers to
establish validity and propriety of payment.

We recommended and the LCE agreed through the GAD Focal Point
personnel to continue observing and employing GAD PPAs in addressing
significant gender issues and concerns within the locality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer continue the timely withholding
and remittance of taxes.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal Human
Resource Management Officer (HRMO) in coordination with the
Municipal Accountant to (a) continuously reconcile records with the GSIS
EBCS; and (b) facilitate coordination between the former Municipal
personnel and the GSIS for reconciliation and settlement of prior years’
unremitted contributions and loan amortizations.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to continuously abide by the National Health Insurance Act on
the withholding and prompt remittance of PhilHealth premiums.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to instruct the Municipal
Accountant to (a) continuously adhere to the requirements of R.A. No.
9679 on the withholding and prompt remittance of HDMF contributions
and loan repayments; and (b) verify and settle the unremitted prior years’
balances.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the: (a) Municipal Engineering Office to regularly furnish the Accounting Office with the project status report for the faithful reporting of the balance of Construction in Progress account; and (b) Municipal Accountant to: (i) reclassify the completed projects to their proper asset accounts based on the updated project implementation status through the following journal entry: Infrastructure Assets account ₱ 4,623,883.13; Buildings and Other Structures ₱ 734,493.00; Construction-in-Progress ₱ 5,358,376.13; (ii) recognize depreciation expense from the year the projects were completed, thus: Prior Year’s Adjustment ₱ 817,655.94; Accumulated Depreciation ₱ 817,655.94; and (iii) henceforth regularly seek an updated status from the Engineering Office of the projects recognized under the CIP accounts as at end of every reporting period.
observations.managementAction: regularly communicate with MAO regarding the status of project implementation. Adjusting entries to reclassify completed projects to its proper PPE accounts were effected by MAO. Depreciation for reclassified PPE accounts were recorded by MAO. The MAO and MEO are coordinating on the updated status of completion of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to require the: (a) Municipal Accountant to: (i) ensure that unexpended balances of the current year’s LDRRMF is transferred to the Trust Fund-DRRM account at yearend; and (ii) reconcile the balances per books and Notes to FS to reflect the accurate amount of the 5% LDRRMF and adjust any identified discrepancies and adjust any accordingly; and (b) LDRRMO to sustain the efficient utilization of the fund through continuous identification and implementation of PPAs geared to increasing the Municipality’s capabilities and resilience in responding and mitigating disasters and calamities.
observations.managementAction: Previous year’s balance of LDRRMF were transferred to Trust Fund. The MAO will make sure to have periodic reconciliation and adjust any variance in order to reflect the accurate balances per books and Notes to FS. The LDRRMO will continue to comply with the efficient utilization of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to: (a) ensure that the 20% DF is consistently utilized optimally; and (b) continuously identify and prioritize development projects aligned with the socio-economic and environmental needs of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU continue to comply with the utilization of 20% as per budgeted PPAs. The LGU to prioritize development projects aligned with the socio-economic and environmental needs of the Municipality. Monitor Local Project Monitoring Committee (LPMC) every quarter regarding the utilization of 20% DF. Formulate a Department Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) that incorporates social, economic, physical, and environmental sectors to align with local needs and aspirations. Conduct Needs Assessment to identify key problem areas, potential, and resources for sustainable development.

observations.managementAction: The LGU complies with the deductions and remittance of contributions for the current year. To temporarily designate employees to handle reconciliation of PhilHealth balances; reconciliation of records between LGU and PhilHealth is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for its effort in the settlement of its outstanding disallowances and recommended to which the LCE agreed to require all officials responsible to ensure that all fiscal transactions of the Municipality comply with pertinent laws and regulations to avoid any suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 | Management generally complied with the relevant laws, rules, and regulations on the receipt and use of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen internal control on collections by (i) segregating incompatible functions in his office; (ii) seeing to it that the Date column of the RCD is filled out to timely monitor the actual collections and deposits; (iii) requiring submission of monthly RAAF; (iv) cause the immediate restitution of the remaining unremitted collections; and (v) henceforth strengthen internal control in the Treasury Office; and (b) file appropriate charges against the former erring employees in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

2.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Property In-Charge to (a) consider hiring or designating additional staff focused on (i) updating, reconciling and maintaining PPE inventory and records; and (ii) coordinating with the concerned Municipal officials for the completion of the RPCPPE; and (b) cause the necessary appraisal of the undocumented PPEs such that identification of the remaining economic age and cost will be determined to provide for the required depreciation expenses.

3.8	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant, Property-In Charge, and RHU officials to (a) designate or hire an additional staff who will be responsible for preparing and updating inventory records and reports; and (b) coordinate and make appropriate strategies and actions with regards to the proper accounting and adequate safeguard of inventories to ensure the availability of medicines and medical supplies in conformance with the rules, regulations and best practices in inventory management.

4.10	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in coordination with the Municipal Engineer to (a) endeavor to further maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund; (b) regularly monitor project implementation status for a timely intervention in case of delays or failure in implementation; and (c) henceforth, continuously assess new developments affecting the socio-economic and environmental status of the community for a more responsive use of the fund.

5.11	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the LDRRMO to (a) continue implementing LDRRM programs and projects that are attuned to the call of the times; and (b) ensure that the PPAs are accurately labeled to reflect the true intent of the activity and thus, preclude misinterpretation by the users of the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) The accountable officers to settle immediately all their cash advances which are due for liquidation; (b) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (i) withhold the salaries of accountable officers who will fail to comply with the directive or demand for liquidation; (ii) ensure the proper granting, utilization and liquidation of all cash advances in accordance with the existing rules and regulations; (iii) intensify monitoring of unliquidated cash advances through sending out regular reminders or demand letters, where warranted; and (iv) request for write off the accounts that already meet the criteria for write off pursuant to COA rules and regulations.

6.7.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Budget Officer to (i) ensure that disbursements are within the budget and advise the Municipal Engineer to request for additional allocation, if needed; and (ii) see to it that other SEF activities are not deprived of funds as a result of augmentation of one SEF activity to another; or sourced from General Fund allocation, if no available sources from SEF; and (b) Municipal Engineer to timely update the concerned officials on its assessment of program of works of small repair projects undertaken by administration chargeable to SEF to see whether the original approved allocation or budget need to be augmented, and act accordingly or request for augmentation or realignment from the LCE through the Budget Officer to avoid disbursements with no budgetary authority within the year in audit.

8.7.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to (a) properly plan for the timely implementation and completion of the projects; (b) regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary actions may be done at the earliest time for optimal implementation of the Development Fund; and (c) be prudent in planning the programs and projects under the Development Fund to achieve the optimal benefits that could be derived therefrom.

9.10.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Local Poverty Reduction and Action Team (LPRAT), as the program’s oversight body to, (i) coordinate with the funding agencies to facilitate satisfaction of their requirements; (ii) closely monitor the project implementation for the timely identification of concerns that may affect the effective and efficient use of the funds received; and (b) Municipal Engineer ensure that the projects are in accordance with the technical specifications required by the funding agencies and as clearly defined in the contract.

10.6.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the MSWDO and the LCPC to prepare a comprehensive plan integrating through attribution of related PPAs; and (b) cause the implementation of the said plan to ensure the promotion of children’s welfare and protection.

11.5.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Municipal Planning Development Officer to (a) post updated reports on the Full Disclosure Bulletin Board; and (b) henceforth observe the timelines in the posting of the required operational reports at the designated places in order to attain maximum transparency in LGU governance.

12.6.	We recommended and management agreed to intensify collection on RPT SET, other regulatory fees and service user charges/service income. Moreover, keep up on its good work of increasing or achieving its revenue collections target.
13.5.	We recommended and management agreed that the Management continue to comply with BIR regulations by remitting all taxes withheld to the BIR.
14.4.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality continue adhering to the requirements of R.A. No. 8291 or the GSIS Act of 1997.

15.3.	We recommended and management agreed that the Municipality continue to comply with the National Health Insurance Act by remitting all contributions withheld to the Philippine Health Insurance Corporation.
16.3.	We recommended and Management agreed to continue adhering to the requirement of R.A. No. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009.

17.5.	We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate settlement of (a) disallowances; otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable in accordance with the COE issued; and (b) Notices of Suspensions to avoid of their maturing into disallowances.

of COA Circular No. 96-011
and immediately take up in
the books the valid       
reconciling items as      
reflected in the BRS and

submitted to the COA      
Auditor should be         
supported by copies of    
bank statement and its

record the acquisition,

and submit the report

(LCE) and other concerned

insurable properties

Local Chief Executive     
cause the immediate

withheld promptly to the

1(c) of Republic Act No.  
7649.

We recommended that the   
management continue to

the GSIS as required by   
Section 52 paragraphs g   
and h of Republic Act No. |
8291.

We recommended the Local  
Chief Executive to require
the immediate settlement  
of disallowances          
otherwise, cause the      
withholding of salaries of
those employees determined
to be liable and comply   
with the requirements to  
settle the Notices of     
Suspensions to avoid of   
their maturity into       
disallowances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and immediately take up in the books the valid reconciling items as reflected in the BRS and henceforth, the BRS submitted to the COA Auditor should be supported by copies of bank statement and its supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the: (i) Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPELC for each class of property to record the acquisition, adjustments and disposal thereof;  and (ii) MTO maintain complete and updated property cards for all PPE purchased in prior and current year and ensure to update them regularly to avoid backlogs;
create an Inventory Committee that shall conduct physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and submit the report thereon to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year; and
instruct the immediate and proper disposal of unserviceable properties to obtain or realize better sale on recoverable value.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that established controls for remittances, deposit of collections and prompt reporting are in place and implemented by the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant. We further recommended that:
Collecting officers should be required to prepare RCD’s at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily, particularly when collections exceed P5,000.00 and the Municipal Treasurer should be directed to deposit the collections intact daily; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer observe strictly the policies on cash collections, deposits and reporting in accordance with accounting policies set forth in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) require the concerned personnel to liquidate/submit liquidation reports as soon as the purpose thereof had been accomplished and refund/return the excess cash advance, if any for the full settlement of cash advance; (b) update and monitor regularly the timely liquidation of cash advances and send demand letters to the accountable/liable officers with long outstanding cash advances; (c) refrain from granting new cash advance to personnel without the proper liquidation of the previous cash advance; (d) cause withholding of the salary of the accountable officer as warranted by circumstances if no liquidation of the cash advance was received; and (e) stop granting cash advances to non-regular employees of the LGU and cause the immediate liquidation of the unsettled cash advances of those who have not yet reported.
We also recommended that the Municipality observe strictly the provision under Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997 and that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) seek the coordination of the DepEd Municipality of Talugtug in requiring the concerned personnel to immediately liquidate/submit liquidation reports and refund/return the excess cash advance, if any for the full settlement of their cash advances; and (b) send demand letters to the accountable/liable officers with long outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that management:
ensure that the 20% development fund is optimally utilized and all projects/programs/activities to be funded shall aim to attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and shall contribute the nature of investment or capital expenditures;
conform with the requirements provided for under COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to ascertain the validity of the claim and to establish the legality of the transactions; and
submit lacking documents as shown in the above table to avoid suspensions in audit.
We recommended that the management refrain from appropriating the LDRRM fund for purposes other than those authorized under Section 21 of RA 10121.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the officer-in-charge of the Solid Waste Management, the MENRO-designate, to continue the close monitoring of barangays’ compliance with the implementation of existing environmental laws and regulations, and  maintain coordination with Barangay Officials to sustain the constituents’ obedience on “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”; and
exercise strong political will to implement the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment required under R.A. No. 9003.

We recommended that Management fully adhere to the provisions contained under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 01  (JMC) issued by the Philippine Commission on Women PCW), Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG), Department of Budget and Management (DBM) and National Economic and Development Authority (NEDA) particularly on the establishment of GAD data base and enactment of GAD Code of the Municipality.

We recommend that the management: (a) require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit regularly the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor vehicles; (b) ensure that all supporting relevant documents pertaining to gasoline/fuel are properly accomplished and transmitted for audit; and (c) establish and implement effective control/process over the use of government vehicle and fuel consumption as provided in COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply consciously with the timely reporting and submission of accounts and financial reports in order to avoid the postponement of the Auditor’s actions relevant to the subject transactions and other post audit activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and other concerned officials exert more effort to obtain records/documents on all real properties of the municipality and to secure adequate insurance for all insurable properties therein.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld promptly to the BIR as required by Section 1(c) of Republic Act No. 7649.
We recommended that the management continue to remit all compulsory contributions withheld to the GSIS as required by Section 52 paragraphs g and h of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the immediate settlement of disallowances otherwise, cause the withholding of salaries of those employees determined to be liable and comply with the requirements to settle the Notices of Suspensions to avoid of their maturity into disallowances.

We recommended and management agreed to comply with the provisions contained under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 to establish reliable and accurate balances of all bank accounts.

We also recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant should maintain subsidiary for each bank account for easy preparation of the monthly bank reconciliation statement. In addition, bank reconciliation statement should be prepared per bank account number and any differences should be scrutinized and appropriate action should be undertaken.
